volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 845 - Section 999)

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: καὶ τοὺς κακοὺς δρᾶν πανταχοῦ κακῶς ἀεί .
sections.translation: and to punish evil-doers wherever found .

sections.label: Section 846
sections.original: δεινόν γε , θνητοῖς ὡς ἅπαντα συμπίτνει ,
sections.translation: It is strange how each extreme meets in human life ! Custom determines even our natural ties , making the most bitter foes friends , and regarding as foes those who formerly were friends .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: ἐγὼ σὲ καὶ σὸν παῖδα καὶ τύχας σέθεν ,
sections.translation: Hecuba , I feel compassion for you and your son and your ill-fortune , as well as for your suppliant gesture , and I would gladly see that impious host pay you this forfeit for the sake of heaven and justice , if I could only find some way to help you

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: στρατῷ τε μὴ δόξαιμι Κασάνδρας χάριν
sections.translation: without appearing to the army to have plotted the death of the Thracian king for Cassandra ’ s sake . For on one point I am assailed by perplexity : the army count this man their friend , the dead their foe ; that he is dear to you

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐστί , χωρὶς τοῦτο κοὐ κοινὸν στρατῷ . —
sections.translation: is a matter apart , in which the army has no share . Reflect on this ; for though you find me ready to share your toil and quick to lend my aid , yet the risk of being reproached by the Achaeans makes me hesitate .

sections.label: Section 864
sections.original: φεῦ .
sections.translation: Ah ! there is not in the world a single man free ;

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: ἢ χρημάτων γὰρ δοῦλός ἐστιν ἢ τύχης ,
sections.translation: for he is a slave either to money or to fortune , or else the people in their thousands or the fear of public prosecution prevents him from following the dictates of his heart .

sections.label: Section 868
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ ταρβεῖς τῷ τʼ ὄχλῳ πλέον νέμεις ,
sections.translation: But since you are afraid , deferring too much to the rabble , I will rid you of that fear .

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: σύνισθι μὲν γάρ , ἤν τι βουλεύσω κακὸν
sections.translation: Thus : be aware of my plot if I devise mischief against this murderer , but refrain from any share in it . And if any uproar or attempt at rescue breaks out among the Achaeans , when the Thracian is suffering his doom , check it without seeming to do so on my account .

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: τὰ δʼ ἄλλα — θάρσει — πάντʼ ἐγὼ θήσω καλῶς .
sections.translation: For what remains — take heart — I will arrange everything well .

sections.label: Section 876
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ; τί δράσεις ; πότερα φάσγανον χερὶ
sections.translation: How ? what will you do ? will you take a sword in your old hand and slay the barbarian , or do you have drugs or some means to aid you ? Who will take your part ? Where will you procure friends ?

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: στέγαι κεκεύθασʼ αἵδε Τρῳάδων ὄχλον .
sections.translation: Sheltered beneath these tents is a crowd of Trojan women .

sections.label: Section 881
sections.original: τὰς αἰχμαλώτους εἶπας , Ἑλλήνων ἄγραν ;
sections.translation: Do you mean the captives , the booty of the Hellenes ?

sections.label: Section 882
sections.original: σὺν ταῖσδε τὸν ἐμὸν φονέα τιμωρήσομαι .
sections.translation: With their help I will punish my murderous foe .

sections.label: Section 883
sections.original: καὶ πῶς γυναιξὶν ἀρσένων ἔσται κράτος ;
sections.translation: How are women to master men ?

sections.label: Section 884
sections.original: δεινὸν τὸ πλῆθος σὺν δόλῳ τε δύσμαχον .
sections.translation: Numbers are a fearful thing , and joined to craft a desperate foe .

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: δεινόν · τὸ μέντοι θῆλυ μέμφομαι γένος .
sections.translation: True ; still I have a mean opinion of the female race .

sections.label: Section 886
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐ γυναῖκες εἷλον Αἰγύπτου τέκνα
sections.translation: What ? did not women slay the sons of Aegyptus , and utterly clear Lemnos of men ? But let it be thus ; put an end to our conference , and send this woman for me safely through the army .

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: γυναῖκα . — καὶ σὺ Θρῃκὶ πλαθεῖσα ξένῳ
sections.translation: To a servant And you are to draw near my Thracian friend and say , Hecuba , once queen of Ilium , summons you , on your own business no less than hers , your children too , for they also must hear what she has to say . The servant goes out . Defer awhile , Agamemnon ,

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: Πολυξένης ἐπίσχες , Ἀγάμεμνον , τάφον ,
sections.translation: the burial of Polyxena lately slain , so that brother and sister may be laid on the same pyre and buried side by side , a double cause of sorrow to their mother .

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ οὕτω · καὶ γὰρ εἰ μὲν ἦν στρατῷ
sections.translation: So shall it be ; yet if the army were able to sail , I could not have granted you this favor ;

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: νῦν δʼ , οὐ γὰρ ἵησʼ οὐρίους πνοὰς θεός ,
sections.translation: but as it is , for the god sends forth no favoring breeze , the army must wait and look for a calm voyage . Good luck to you , for this is the interest alike of individual and state , that the wrong-doer be punished and the good man prosper . Agamemnon departs as Hecuba withdraws into the tent .

sections.label: Section 1.905
sections.original: σὺ μέν , ὦ πατρὶς Ἰλιάς ,
sections.translation: No more , my native Ilium , shall you be counted among the towns never sacked ; so thick a cloud of Hellene troops is settling all around , wasting you with the spear ;

sections.label: Section 1.910
sections.original: ἀπὸ δὲ στεφάναν κέκαρ-
sections.translation: you are shorn of your crown of towers , and fouled most piteously with filthy soot ; no more , ah me ! shall I tread your streets .

sections.label: Section 1.914
sections.original: μεσονύκτιος ὠλλύμαν ,
sections.translation: It was in the middle of the night my ruin came ,

sections.label: Section 1.915
sections.original: ἦμος ἐκ δείπνων ὕπνος ἡδὺς ἐπʼ ὄσσοις
sections.translation: in the hour when sleep steals sweetly over the eyes after the feast is done . After the music and dancing , my husband had brought the sacrifice to an end and was lying in our bridal-chamber ,

sections.label: Section 1.920
sections.original: το , ξυστὸν δʼ ἐπὶ πασσάλῳ ,
sections.translation: his spear hung on a peg ; with never a thought of the sailor-throng encamped upon the Trojan shores .

sections.label: Section 2.923
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ πλόκαμον ἀναδέτοις
sections.translation: And I was braiding my tresses beneath a tight-drawn head-band

sections.label: Section 2.925
sections.original: χρυσέων ἐνόπτρων λεύσ-
sections.translation: before my golden mirror ’ s countless rays , so that I might lie down to rest ; when through the city rose a din , and a cry went ringing down the streets of Troy : You

sections.label: Section 2.930
sections.original: ας τόδʼ · Ὦ
sections.translation: sons of Hellas , when , oh ! when will you sack the citadel of Ilium , and seek your homes ?

sections.label: Section 2.933
sections.original: λέχη δὲ φίλια μονόπεπλος
sections.translation: I left my bed , wearing only a tunic , like a Dorian girl ,

sections.label: Section 2.935
sections.original: σεμνὰν προσίζουσʼ οὐκ
sections.translation: and sought in vain , ah me ! to station myself at the holy hearth of Artemis ; for , after seeing my husband slain , I was led away over the broad sea ; with many a backward look at my city ,

sections.label: Section 2.940
sections.original: νόστιμον
sections.translation: when the ship began her homeward voyage and parted me from Ilium ’ s strand ; till alas ! for very grief I fainted ,

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: τὰν τοῖν Διοσκούροιν Ἑλέναν κάσιν
sections.translation: Cursing Helen the sister of the Dioscuri , and Paris the baleful shepherd of Ida ;

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: αἰνόπαριν κατάρᾳ
sections.translation: for it was their marriage , which was no marriage but misery sent by some demon , that robbed me of my country and drove me from my home .

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: ἃν μήτε πέλαγος ἅλιον ἀπαγάγοι πάλιν ,
sections.translation: Oh ! may the sea ’ s salt flood never carry her home again ; and may she never set foot in her father ’ s halls !

sections.label: Section 953
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ἀνδρῶν Πρίαμε , φιλτάτη δὲ σύ ,
sections.translation: My dear friend Priam , and you no less , Hecuba , I weep to see you and your city thus , and your daughter lately slain .

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: τήν τʼ ἀρτίως θανοῦσαν ἔκγονον σέθεν .
sections.translation: Ah ! there is nothing to be relied on ; fair fame is insecure , nor is there any guarantee that prosperity will not be turned to woe . For the gods confound our fortunes , tossing them to and fro , and introduce confusion , so that our perplexity

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: σέβωμεν αὐτούς . ἀλλὰ ταῦτα μὲν τί δεῖ
sections.translation: may make us worship them . But what use is it to lament these things , and make no advance ahead of trouble ? If you are blaming me at all for my absence , stop a moment ; I happened to be away in the very heart of Thrace when you came here ; but on my return ,

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: ἤδη πόδʼ ἔξω δωμάτων αἴροντί μοι
sections.translation: just as I was starting from my home for the same purpose , your maid fell in with me , and gave me your message , which brought me here at once .

sections.label: Section 968
sections.original: αἰσχύνομαί σε προσβλέπειν ἐναντίον ,
sections.translation: Polymestor , I am held in such wretchedness that I blush to meet your eye ;

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ὅτῳ γὰρ ὤφθην εὐτυχοῦσʼ , αἰδώς μʼ ἔχει
sections.translation: for my present evil case makes me ashamed to face you who saw me in happier days , and I could not look on you with unfaltering gaze . Do not then think it ill-will towards you , Polymestor ; there is another cause as well ,

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: γυναῖκας ἀνδρῶν μὴ βλέπειν ἐναντίον .
sections.translation: I mean the custom which forbids women to meet men ’ s gaze .

sections.label: Section 976
sections.original: καὶ θαῦμά γʼ οὐδέν . ἀλλὰ τίς χρεία σʼ ἐμοῦ ;
sections.translation: No wonder , surely . But what need do you have of me ? Why did you send for me to come here from my house ?

sections.label: Section 978
sections.original: ἴδιον ἐμαυτῆς δή τι πρὸς σὲ βούλομαι
sections.translation: I wish to tell you and your children a private matter of my own ; please bid

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: χωρὶς κέλευσον τῶνδʼ ἀποστῆναι δόμων .
sections.translation: your attendants withdraw from the tent .

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: χωρεῖτʼ · ἐν ἀσφαλεῖ γὰρ ἥδʼ ἐρημία .
sections.translation: Retire ; this desert spot is safe enough . The guards go out ; to Hecuba You are my friend , and this Achaean army is well-disposed to me . But you must tell me how prosperity

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: φίλοις ἐπαρκεῖν ; ὡς ἕτοιμός εἰμʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: is to help its unlucky friends ; for I am ready to do so .

sections.label: Section 986
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν εἰπὲ παῖδʼ ὃν ἐξ ἐμῆς χερὸς
sections.translation: First tell me of the child Polydorus , whom you are keeping in your house , received from me and his father ; is he alive ? The rest I will ask you after that .

sections.label: Section 989
sections.original: μάλιστα · τοὐκείνου μὲν εὐτυχεῖς μέρος .
sections.translation: Yes , you still have a share in fortune there .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθʼ , ὡς εὖ κἀξίως λέγεις σέθεν .
sections.translation: Well said , dear friend ! how worthy of you !

sections.label: Section 991
sections.original: τί δῆτα βούλῃ δεύτερον μαθεῖν ἐμοῦ ;
sections.translation: What next would you learn of me ?

sections.label: Section 992
sections.original: εἰ τῆς τεκούσης τῆσδε ⋯ μέμνηταί τί μου ;
sections.translation: has he any recollection of me his mother ?

sections.label: Section 993
sections.original: καὶ δεῦρό γʼ ὡς σὲ κρύφιος ἐζήτει μολεῖν .
sections.translation: Yes , he was longing to steal away here to you .

sections.label: Section 994
sections.original: χρυσὸς δὲ σῶς ὃν ἦλθεν ἐκ Τροίας ἔχων ;
sections.translation: Is the gold safe , which he brought with him from Troy ?

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: σῶς , ἐν δόμοις γε τοῖς ἐμοῖς φρουρούμενος .
sections.translation: Safe under lock and key in my halls .

sections.label: Section 996
sections.original: σῶσόν νυν αὐτὸν μηδʼ ἔρα τῶν πλησίον .
sections.translation: Do save it , but do not desire your neighbor ’ s goods .

sections.label: Section 997
sections.original: ἥκιστʼ · ὀναίμην τοῦ παρόντος , ὦ γύναι .
sections.translation: Not I ; may I benefit by what I have , lady !

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ἃ λέξαι σοί τε καὶ παισὶν θέλω ;
sections.translation: Do you know what I wish to say to you and your children ?

sections.label: Section 999
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα · τῷ σῷ τοῦτο σημανεῖς λόγῳ .
sections.translation: No ; your words will declare it .
